section13_checks.prohibition_description: Grant Agreement Annex templates must not be used as the governing structural schema for proposal writing; application form templates (Tier 2A) govern proposal writing
section13_checks.check_reasoning: The artifact carries schema_id 'orch.tier5.implementation_section.v1'. Its traceability_footer.primary_sources[] identifies docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/extracted/section_schema_registry.json as the structural authority, which is the correct Tier 2A instrument schema source. No reference to any 'Grant Agreement Annex', 'AGA Template', 'Model Grant Agreement template', or 'Annex [0-9]' used as a structural schema source was found in either the sub_sections content or the traceability_footer.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, and expected impacts must not be invented; all must be traceable to Tier 2B source documents
section13_checks.check_reasoning: The artifact references specific call scope requirements and constraints: SR-07 (demonstrator validation scope), SR-09 (ADRA/GenAI4EU Hub liaison), SR-10 (AI-on-demand platform integration), CC-03, CC-04, CC-05, CC-07. Claim statuses CS-12 and CS-13 confirm Tier 2B sourcing for these. The traceability_footer.primary_sources[] includes docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json and docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json — both within docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/. All call-constraint claims in the artifact carry Tier 2B source references.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, equipment — must not be invented; all must be traceable to Tier 3 sources
section13_checks.check_reasoning: The artifact names eight specific partners with full legal names, country codes, organisation types, capability descriptions, and WP leadership assignments: ATU, BIIS, CERIA, NIHS, ISIA, ELI (described as operating across over 80 countries), BAL, and FIIT. Claim CS-11 (status: confirmed) records 'Partner legal names, countries, organisation types, expertise from Tier 3' with source_ref 'Tier 3: consortium/partners.json'. The traceability_footer.primary_sources[] includes docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json and docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json, both within docs/tier3_project_instantiation/. All partner-specific claims carry Tier 3 source references.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: No Phase 8 activity — including preparatory drafting — may commence before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed; absent budget artifacts are a blocking gate failure
section13_checks.check_reasoning: The artifact is a Tier 5 Phase 8 deliverable (schema_id 'orch.tier5.implementation_section.v1'). Claim CS-02 (status: confirmed) asserts 'Budget gate passed; gate_pass_declaration=pass; no blocking inconsistencies' with source_ref 'Tier 4: budget_gate_assessment.json, gate_pass_declaration'. The traceability_footer.primary_sources[] references docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json. The content contains the phrase 'All nine WPs are resourced per the validated budget' — a general reference to the validated budget gate rather than an independently asserted budget figure. No specific unvalidated budget figures (€ amounts, EUR amounts, or person-month counts presented as confirmed values) appear in the sub_sections content. Budget gate passage is affirmed by the artifact's own confirmed claim_status.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Durable decisions must not be held only in agent memory; decisions affecting future interpretation, traceability, or reproducibility must be written to Tier 4
section13_checks.check_reasoning: This prohibition governs agent memory management and is not checkable from the content of a single Tier 5 proposal section artifact. The artifact provides no visibility into whether any runtime agent held durable decisions exclusively in memory. Enforcement of this prohibition is the responsibility of the decision-log-update skill and the runtime execution layer. Per the skill specification (§2, Check 13.5), check_status is recorded as 'pass' on the grounds of structural non-applicability to artifact-level inspection.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Skills must not become de facto constitutional authorities by expanding their scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic
section13_checks.check_reasoning: This prohibition governs skill scope behaviour at the system architecture level and is not checkable from reading the content of a Tier 5 proposal section artifact. The artifact's content does not reflect skill scope definitions. Per the skill specification (§2, Check 13.6), check_status is recorded as 'pass' on the grounds of structural non-applicability to artifact-level inspection.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Phases must not be silently reordered; gate conditions must not be weakened to allow workflow progress when upstream inputs are incomplete
section13_checks.check_reasoning: The artifact is a Phase 8 Tier 5 deliverable. Its traceability_footer.primary_sources[] references only upstream phase outputs: phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json (Phase 3), phase4_gantt_milestones/gantt.json (Phase 4), phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json (Phase 6), and phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json (Phase 7). All referenced phases are upstream of Phase 8. No forward references to Phase 8 outputs used as inputs are present, and no evidence of circular dependency or phase reordering is detectable in the artifact.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Proposal text must not be finalised when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable
section13_checks.check_reasoning: The artifact's validation_status.overall_status is 'inferred', not 'unresolved'. Inspection of all 15 claim_statuses (CS-01 through CS-15) confirms that 14 are 'confirmed' and 1 is 'inferred' (CS-09: ethics workstreams, inferred from Tier 4 implementation_architecture.json ethics_assessment.issues[ETHICS-01/02/03] with stated source_ref and inference basis). No claim_status carries status 'unresolved'. No sub_sections content contains '[DATA REQUIRED: ...]', 'INCOMPLETE:', or equivalent gap-flagging language — which is consistent and correct given that no claims are unresolved. The condition triggering a violation (unresolved status present but not explicitly flagged) is not present.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules must not be substituted for Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible
section13_checks.check_reasoning: The artifact contains two potential regulatory references: (1) 'ATU coordinates European Commission reporting and ADRA/GenAI4EU Hub liaison (SR-09, CC-03, CC-04)' — this describes a project coordination activity and attributes it to Tier 2B call constraints SR-09/CC-03/CC-04; it does not assert what the Horizon Europe grant agreement legally requires. (2) 'EU AI Act compliance (RISK-08, medium/medium; auditability by design)' — this is an entry in the project risk register treating external EU legislation as a project compliance risk, not an assertion of a Horizon Europe programme participation condition, grant agreement term, or financial regulation. The traceability_footer does not include a Tier 1 source path, but no claim in the artifact qualifies as a direct assertion of Horizon Europe legal rules (participation conditions, grant agreement terms, financial regulation) sourced from agent knowledge rather than Tier 1 documents.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Tier 5 outputs must be traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1–4; outputs not traceable to higher tiers are constitutionally prohibited
section13_checks.check_reasoning: The artifact is a Tier 5 deliverable. traceability_footer.no_unsupported_claims_declaration is true. All 15 claim_statuses (CS-01 through CS-15) have either 'confirmed' or 'inferred' status — none are 'unresolved' lacking a source_ref. The traceability_footer.primary_sources[] provides 10 source path entries spanning Tier 2A (section_schema_registry.json), Tier 2B (scope_requirements.json, call_constraints.json), Tier 3 (partners.json, roles.json, selected_call.json), and Tier 4 (wp_structure.json, gantt.json, implementation_architecture.json, budget_gate_assessment.json). The condition triggering a violation (unresolved claims with no source_ref) is not present.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents must not be modified to reflect project-specific assumptions
section13_checks.check_reasoning: This prohibition governs whether Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents were modified as part of producing the artifact. It is not checkable by reading the Tier 5 proposal section itself, which is an output artifact and provides no visibility into whether source documents were altered. Per the skill specification (§2, Check 13.11), check_status is recorded as 'pass' on the grounds of structural non-applicability to artifact-level inspection.
